title: Hurst

{{Quote|Hurst always said you were no good. He knew there was something wrong with you… Bane.|Gerd to Dessel|<ref name="PoD" />}}
'''Hurst''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] [[Miner/Legends|miner]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Apatros/Legends|Apatros]]. [[Hurst's wife]] [[Death/Legends|died]] giving birth to their son, [[Darth Bane/Legends|Dessel]], which led to Hurst resenting his son and blaming him for his life's problems, specifically the death of his [[Marriage/Legends|wife]] and being stuck working on Apatros. Hurst gave Dessel the nickname "Bane" and often beat his son. In [[1008 BBY]], Hurst died of a suspected massive [[Heart/Legends|heart]] attack, which was actually Dessel unknowingly crushing his heart with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Eventually, his son went on to become the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Bane, who would not realize he had accidentally killed his father until [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later.

==Biography==
===Parenting a Sith Lord===
{{Quote|He raised you by himself after your mama died, and you don't even have the respect to call him Dad?|Gred complaining about Dessel's attitude|<ref name="PoD" />}}
[[File:DarthBane-TEA.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Hurst's son eventually grew up to become the Sith Lord Darth Bane.]]
Hurst was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Apatros/Legends|Apatros]]. He eventually began working at the local [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] [[Mining/Legends|mine]] owned by [[Outer Rim Oreworks Company]]. Hurst was [[Marriage/Legends|married]], and,<ref name="PoD" /> around [[1026 BBY]],<ref name="DeathDate" /> his [[Hurst's wife|wife]] gave birth to their son, [[Darth Bane/Legends|Dessel]]. Hurst's wife [[Death/Legends|died]] giving birth to Dessel, and Hurst was left to raise the child on his own. He subsequently blamed Dessel for all his troubles: his wife's death and being stuck working in the cortosis mine on Apatros. Hurst referred to his son as "Bane," for he considered Dessel to be the bane of his existence. Hurst was a heavy drinker after his wife's death and often beat Dessel while intoxicated.<ref name="PoD" />

His son began working in the cortosis mine at the age of thirteen, and other [[Miner/Legends|miners]], including Hurst's good friend [[Gerd]], often tormented and bullied Dessel. Hurst spent more time [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]] and drinking than working, and often had to borrow [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] from Outer Rim Oreworks to pay for his monthly room and board. The interest rate on the loans piled up [[Standard year/Legends|year]] after year, and Hurst accumulated great debt.<ref name="PoD" />

===Death and legacy===
{{Quote|Five years ago today your own father died, and you don't even remember!|Gerd, to Dessel about the latter's father death|<ref name="PoD" />}}
In [[1008 BBY]],<ref name="DeathDate" /> Hurst died of what was claimed to be a massive [[Heart/Legends|heart]] attack after beating his son heavily in a drunken rage. After his death, Dessel was stuck with his father's debt. Dessel surmised that Hurst, who resented his son, would be happy that he was stuck with the bill.<ref name="PoD" /> In [[1003 BBY]],<ref name="DeathDate" /> Gerd reprimanded Dessel because of his lack of grief in remembering his father's death. The two started arguing after Dessel answered that he did not, in fact, miss his father, which eventually led to a fight that Dessel won using [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] instinctively.<ref name="PoD" />

[[File:2nd_Battle_of_Ambria.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Darth Bane remembered his father during the Duel on Ambria (pictured).]]
Sometime later, Dessel escaped Apatros, joining the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Army/Legends|army]] during the [[New Sith Wars]] against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. After serving with distinction, Dessel's [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitivity]] was discovered and he became [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Bane, a powerful [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]. Darth Bane named himself after the nickname "Bane" given to him by Hurst.<ref name="PoD" /> Later on, as a Sith, Bane often thought about his father's beatings<ref name="RoT">''[[Darth Bane: Rule of Two]]''</ref> and eventually realized that he unknowingly caused Hurst's death with the Force.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|68|Hurst}}</ref> 

In [[980 BBY]],<ref name="DateDoE">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Darth Bane: Dynasty of Evil]]'', which include the [[Duel on Ambria (980 BBY)|Duel on Ambria]], to [[980 BBY]].</ref> Darth Bane [[Duel on Ambria (980 BBY)|fought]] against his Sith apprentice [[Darth Zannah/Legends|Darth Zannah]] on the planet [[Ambria/Legends|Ambria]]. During the fight, Zannah used [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith sorcery]] against Bane, making him experience his worst [[Summon fear|fears]], which included the memory of his father. Bane was eventually able to break the spell after defeating the traumatic memory of Hurst and his beatings.<ref name="DoE">''[[Darth Bane: Dynasty of Evil]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Can't say I miss Hurst the same way you do, Gerd.|Dessel answers to Gerd's scolding|<ref name="PoD" />}}
Hurst greatly resented his son Dessel, blaming him for his wife's death in childbirth and most of his life's problems, such as being stuck working on Apatros. He drank heavily and was a gambler, and he often beat Dessel while drunk. Hurst incited great anger in his son due to the beatings and him giving Dessel the moniker "Bane,"<ref name="PoD" /> which led to his death when Dessel killed him with the Force.<ref name="CSWE" /> Dessel despised Hurst for his way of living and, after Hurst's death, stopped referring to him as his father.<ref name="PoD" /> 

==Behind the scenes==
Hurst first appeared in flashbacks in ''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'', a [[2006]] novel written by [[Drew Karpyshyn]] as the first installment in the [[Star Wars: Darth Bane Trilogy|''Darth Bane Trilogy'']].<ref name="PoD" />
